<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net10.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Exclude the nested xUnit test project so the web app build does not compile test sources. -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Remove="TestProject.Tests/**" />
    <None Remove="TestProject.Tests/**" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <!-- SPA build integration.

       The frontend lives in /src (*.ts) and is compiled to /wwwroot/dist by the
       `npm run build` script (tsc + scripts/copy-assets.mjs). Since wwwroot/dist is
       gitignored, it must be (re)generated before the C# build so the static files
       are present for `UseStaticFiles`/publish. These targets wire npm into the
       MSBuild pipeline so `dotnet build` and `dotnet publish` are self-contained.

       NpmInstall is incremental: it only runs when package.json or package-lock.json
       change (or node_modules is absent). node_modules/.package-lock.json is npm's
       own stamp file and serves as the output/up-to-date marker. -->
  <Target Name="NpmInstall"
          Inputs="package.json;package-lock.json"
          Outputs="node_modules/.package-lock.json">
    <Exec Command="npm install" WorkingDirectory="$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)" />
  </Target>

  <!-- NpmBuild always runs (tsc is fast and honors noEmitOnError), but only after
       deps are in place. Runs before BeforeBuild so dist exists prior to CoreBuild
       and static-asset copy during publish. -->
  <Target Name="NpmBuild"
          DependsOnTargets="NpmInstall"
          BeforeTargets="BeforeBuild">
    <Exec Command="npm run build" WorkingDirectory="$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)" />
  </Target>

</Project>

```csharp
internal static class RecordContract
{
    /// Verifies type is a sealed record reference type in the given namespace.
    public static void AssertSealedRecordInNamespace(Type type, string expectedNamespace)
    {
        Assert.True(type.IsClass, $"{type.Name} must be a reference type (class).");
        Assert.True(type.IsSealed, $"{type.Name} must be sealed.");
        Assert.Equal(expectedNamespace, type.Namespace);
        Assert.True(IsRecord(type), $"{type.Name} must be a record.");
    }

    // A record synthesizes a protected copy constructor and a <Clone>$ method.
    private static bool IsRecord(Type type) =>
        type.GetMethod("<Clone>$", BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.NonPublic) is not null;

    /// Verifies the primary constructor's parameter names/types/order and that
    /// each maps to a public init-only property of the same name and type, with
    /// no extra public properties.
    public static void AssertPositionalContract(Type type, params (string Name, Type Type)[] expected)
    {
        var ctors = type.GetConstructors();
        var primary = Assert.Single(ctors);  // exactly one public constructor
        var parameters = primary.GetParameters();
        Assert.Equal(expected.Length, parameters.Length);
        for (var i = 0; i < expected.Length; i++)
        {
            Assert.Equal(expected[i].Name, parameters[i].Name);
            Assert.Equal(expected[i].Type, parameters[i].ParameterType);
        }

        // exactly the expected public properties, each init-only
        var props = type.GetProperties(BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance);
        Assert.Equal(expected.Length, props.Length);
        foreach (var (name, type2) in expected)
        {
            var prop = type.GetProperty(name, BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance);
            Assert.NotNull(prop);
            Assert.True(prop!.CanRead, $"{name} must be readable.");
            Assert.True(prop.CanWrite, $"{name} must be writable (init).");
            Assert.Equal(type2, prop.PropertyType);
        }
    }
}
```

```csharp
using System.Reflection;
using Xunit;

namespace TestProject.Tests.TestHelpers;

/// <summary>
/// Reflection-based assertions that pin down the compiled contract of a
/// positional record type. Used by the Models DTO characterization tests so
/// that splitting <c>Models/FileSystemModels.cs</c> into per-DTO files is
/// provably behavior-preserving: the namespace, sealed-record kind, primary
/// constructor signature (parameter names, types, and order), and the set of
/// public init-only properties must be unchanged regardless of which file a
/// type is declared in.
/// </summary>
internal static class RecordContract
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Asserts <paramref name="type"/> is a sealed reference-type record that
    /// lives in <paramref name="expectedNamespace"/>.
    /// </summary>
    public static void AssertSealedRecordInNamespace(Type type, string expectedNamespace)
    {
        Assert.True(type.IsClass, $"{type.Name} must be a reference type (class), not a struct.");
        Assert.True(type.IsSealed, $"{type.Name} must be sealed.");
        Assert.False(type.IsAbstract, $"{type.Name} must not be abstract (static).");
        Assert.Equal(expectedNamespace, type.Namespace);
        Assert.True(IsRecord(type),
            $"{type.Name} must be a record (expected a synthesized copy method).");
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// True when the type has the compiler-synthesized record copy method
    /// (<c><Clone>$</c>), which only records and record structs emit.
    /// </summary>
    private static bool IsRecord(Type type) =>
        type.GetMethod("<Clone>$", BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.NonPublic) is not null;

    /// <summary>
    /// Asserts the type declares exactly one public instance constructor whose
    /// parameters match <paramref name="expected"/> in name, type, and order,
    /// AND that each parameter is surfaced as a public instance property of
    /// the same name and type, with no additional public properties. This pins
    /// the positional-record shape (constructor + auto-properties) so a moved
    /// DTO cannot silently gain, lose, reorder, rename, or retype a field.
    /// </summary>
    public static void AssertPositionalContract(Type type, params (string Name, Type Type)[] expected)
    {
        var constructors = type.GetConstructors();
        var primary = Assert.Single(constructors,
            $"{type.Name} must declare exactly one public constructor (the positional primary constructor).");

        var parameters = primary.GetParameters();
        Assert.Equal(expected.Length, parameters.Length);
        for (var i = 0; i < expected.Length; i++)
        {
            Assert.Equal(expected[i].Name, parameters[i].Name);
            Assert.Equal(expected[i].Type, parameters[i].ParameterType);
        }

        var properties = type.GetProperties();
        Assert.Equal(expected.Length, properties.Length);
        foreach (var (name, propertyType) in expected)
        {
            var property = type.GetProperty(name);
            Assert.NotNull(property);
            Assert.True(property!.CanRead, $"{type.Name}.{name} must have a getter.");
            Assert.True(property.CanWrite, $"{type.Name}.{name} must have a setter/init.");
            Assert.Equal(propertyType, property.PropertyType);
        }
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Asserts record value-equality semantics for two instances: equality,
    /// the <c>==</c>/<c>!=</c> operators, and hash-code agreement, all
    /// consistent with <paramref name="expectedEqual"/>.
    /// </summary>
    public static void AssertEquality<T>(T a, T b, bool expectedEqual)
    {
        Assert.Equal(expectedEqual, a!.Equals(b));
        Assert.Equal(expectedEqual, b!.Equals(a));
        // ReSharper disable once PossibleNullReferenceException
        Assert.Equal(expectedEqual, EqualityComparer<T>.Default.Equals(a, b));

        if (expectedEqual)
        {
            Assert.Equal(a!.GetHashCode(), b!.GetHashCode());
        }
    }
}
```

## Summary

I wrote **characterization tests** that pin down the observable contracts of the 5 DTOs currently in `Models/FileSystemModels.cs`, so that splitting them into per-DTO files is provably behavior-preserving. These tests pass against the existing single-file code (the characterization requirement) and will continue to pass after a correct split — but will fail if the implementer breaks any type's shape.

**Files created (test-only):**
- `TestProject.Tests/TestHelpers/RecordContract.cs` — shared reflection helper asserting a type is a sealed record in a given namespace, with an exact positional-constructor signature (param names/types/order), the matching public property set, and record value-equality semantics.
- `TestProject.Tests/Models/FileEntryDtoTests.cs`, `BrowseResultDtoTests.cs`, `SearchResultDtoTests.cs`, `MoveRequestTests.cs`, `CopyRequestTests.cs` — per-DTO tests covering: sealed-record-in-namespace, exact ctor/property contract, constructor→property assignment, value equality (equal / each-field-differs / null / self / `==`/`!=`), `with` copy semantics, deconstruction, and synthesized `ToString`. The collection DTOs additionally pin reference-based collection comparison and `null` vs `""` `Parent`; the request DTOs pin that `MoveRequest`/`CopyRequest` remain distinct, non-interchangeable types.

**What the tests would catch (and thus protect the refactor against):** renamed/reordered/retyped/added/removed fields, namespace changes, dropping `sealed`, converting a record to a class/struct, altering value equality, or merging `MoveRequest`/`CopyRequest`.

**Deliberately omitted:** file-existence structural assertions (e.g. "5 files exist, `FileSystemModels.cs` deleted") — these cannot pass against the current single-file code (violating the characterization principle), and duplicate type declarations across files would already fail `dotnet build`. XML-doc-comment assertions are also omitted: the current code has none (would fail before the change) and docs aren't observable runtime behavior.

**Dead/useless tests spotted:** None — the reviewed existing tests (`FileServiceOptionsTests`, `ProgramWiringTests`, `FilesControllerTests`, `FakeFileService`) are meaningful and assert real outcomes.
